comments: Phase 1 pilot authorizations have one year from authorization to fully address this process but must demonstrate continuous quarterly progress.
Phase 2 Pilot participants must demonstrate significant progress towards addressing this process prior to submission for authorization review.

releases.description: Consolidated Rules for 2026 (official launch). Key Security Indicators renumbered to mnemonic IDs; the AFR (Authorization by FedRAMP) family was retired and TPR (Third-Party) was renamed SCR (Supply Chain Risk). Rebuilt from the published markdown corpus by scripts/build-ksi-catalog.py.

This release updates a significant number of KSIs to improve clarity and expectations; in general the measures required to meet each KSI remain unchanged but these updates should make it easier to address them. In additional, some KSIs have been retired.
No effective/material changes; adjusted some KSI-AFR indcators to reference "process" instead of "standard" and added a note to KSI-AFR-02 addressing the potential infinite loop.
No material changes to content; updated JSON structure with additional information about Rev5 application added.

Updates Key Security Indicators for the FedRAMP 20x Phase Two pilot, including underlying structural changes to machine-readable docs; Renamed KSI "indicator" property to "theme" and KSI "requirements" property to "indicators" to match current naming conventions..

related_rfcs.full_name: FedRAMP RFC-0015: Recommended Secure Configuration Standard

releases.description: Minor updates to improve clarity; switch from federal information to federal customer data; add impact level metadata; no substantive changes.
Minor non-breaking updates to align term definitions and highlighted terms across updated materials (no changes to KSIs, definitions are now in FRD-ALL).
Key Security Indicators in this release are unchanged from previously releases. 25.05C adds references for each KSI to underlying SP 800-53 controls.

Initial release of Key Security Indicators from 25.05; the previous 25.05A release contained errors during conversion to JSON that are fixed in this release, the KSIs should now be identical to the original 25.05 paper release of the KSIs released on 2025-05-30. FRR-KSI-AY rules were converted to FRR-KSI rules, but otherwise unchanged.
Initial release of Key Security Indicators

related_rfcs.full_name: FedRAMP RFC-0006: 20x Phase One Key Security Indicators

authority.description: Appendix I states "Agencies may also develop overlays for specific types of information or communities of interest (e.g., all web-based applications, all health care-related systems) as part of the security control selection process. Overlays provide a specification of security or privacy controls, control enhancements, supplemental guidance, and other supporting information as part of the tailoring process, that is intended to complement (and further refine) security control baselines. The overlay may be more stringent or less stringent than the original security control baseline and can be applied to multiple information systems."

Section 2.5 states "As the number of controls in [SP 800-53] grows in response to an increasingly sophisticated threat space, it is important for organizations to have the ability to describe key capabilities needed to protect organizational missions and business functions, and to subsequently select controls that—if properly designed, developed, and implemented—produce such capabilities. The use of capabilities simplifies how the protection problem is viewed conceptually. Using the construct of a capability provides a method of grouping controls that are employed for a common purpose or to achieve a common objective." This section later states "Ultimately, authorization decisions (i.e., risk acceptance decisions) are made based on the degree to which the desired capabilities have been effectively achieved."

Section 3.5 states "When organizations employ the concept of capabilities, automated and manual assessments account for all security and privacy controls that comprise the security and privacy capabilities. Assessors are aware of how the controls work together to provide such capabilities."

requires that the Administrator of the General Services Administration shall "in consultation with the [DHS] Secretary, develop, coordinate, and implement a process to support agency review, reuse, and standardization, where appropriate, of security assessments of cloud computing products and services..." 44 USC § 3609 (c) (2) further states that "the [GSA] Administrator shall establish a means for the automation of security assessments and reviews."
authority.delegation: These responsibilities are delegated to the FedRAMP Director

front_matter.purpose: Modern cloud services use automated or code-driven configuration management and control planes to ensure predictable, repeatable, reliable, and secure outcomes during deployment and operation. The majority of a service security assessment can take place continuously via automated validation for simple cloud-native services if the need for a traditional control-by-control narrative approach is removed.
expected_outcomes: Cloud service providers following commercial security best practices will be able to meet and validate FedRAMP security requirements with the application of simple changes and automated capabilities
Third-party independent assessors will have a simpler framework to assess security and implementation decisions based on engineering decisions in context

Federal agencies will be able to easily, quickly, and effectively review and consume security information about the service to make informed risk-based authorization to operate decisions based on their planned use case

indicators.name: Reviewing All Training
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of relevant cybersecurity education and training is persistently reviewed, including at least general training for all employees, role-specific training for employees in high risk roles, training for development and engineering staff on secure software delivery, and training for staff involved with incident response or disaster recovery.

indicators.name: Logging Changes
indicators.statement: Modifications to the cloud service offering are logged and monitored.

controls.title: Testing, Validation, and Documentation of Changes

indicators.name: Redeploying vs Modifying
indicators.statement: Changes to machine-based information resources are executed through the redeployment of version controlled resources rather than direct modification wherever reasonable.

indicators.name: Reviewing Change Procedures
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of documented change management procedures is persistently reviewed.

controls.title: Testing, Validation, and Documentation of Changes

indicators.name: Validating Throughout Deployment
indicators.statement: Persistent testing and validation of changes throughout deployment is automated.

controls.title: Testing, Validation, and Documentation of Changes

indicators.name: Defining Functionality and Privileges
indicators.statement: The functionality and privileges for infrastructure and services are strictly defined.

indicators.name: Enforcing Intended State
indicators.statement: Automated services are used to persistently assess the security of all machine-based information resources and automatically enforce their intended operational state.

indicators.name: Implementing Best Practices
indicators.statement: The use and configuration of third-party machine-based information resources is persistently compared against the original provider's best practices and guidance.

indicators.name: Minimizing Attack Surface
indicators.statement: Machine-based information resources are persistently reviewed to ensure they have a minimal attack surface and that lateral movement is minimized if compromised.

controls.title: Deny by Default — Allow by Exception
Route Traffic to Authenticated Proxy Servers

indicators.name: Optimizing for Availability
indicators.statement: Machine-based information resources are persistently reviewed to ensure they are appropriately optimized for high availability and rapid recovery.

indicators.name: Restricting Network Traffic
indicators.statement: Machine-based information resources are persistently reviewed to ensure they are appropriately configured to limit inbound and outbound network traffic.

controls.title: Deny by Default — Allow by Exception

indicators.name: Reviewing Protections
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of protection against denial of service attacks and other unwanted activity for machine-based information resources is persistently reviewed.

indicators.name: Using Logical Networking
indicators.statement: Logical networking and related capabilities are used and persistently reviewed to enforce traffic flow controls.

indicators.name: Automating Account Management
indicators.statement: The lifecycle and privileges of all accounts, roles, and groups are securely managed using automation.

controls.title: Automated Temporary and Emergency Account Management

indicators.name: Adopting Passwordless Methods
indicators.statement: Secure passwordless methods are used for user authentication and authorization when feasible, otherwise strong passwords with phishing-resistant MFA is used.

indicators.name: Ensuring Least Privilege
indicators.statement: Identity and access management measures are used and persistently reviewed to ensure each user or device can only access the resources they need.

controls.title: Permitted Actions Without Identification or Authentication
Protection of Confidentiality and Integrity Using Encryption
Configure Systems and Components for High-risk Areas
Secure Name/Address Resolution Service (Recursive or Caching Resolver)
Architecture and Provisioning for Name/Address Resolution Service

indicators.name: Authorizing Just-in-Time
indicators.statement: A least-privileged, role and attribute-based, and just-in-time security authorization model is used and persistently reviewed for all user and non-user accounts and services.

controls.title: Automated Temporary and Emergency Account Management
Prohibit Non-privileged Users from Executing Privileged Functions
Access by Subset of Privileged Users
Separation of System and User Functionality

indicators.name: Securing Non-User Authentication
indicators.statement: Appropriately secure authentication methods are used and persistently reviewed for non-user accounts and services.

controls.title: Automated Temporary and Emergency Account Management

indicators.name: Responding to Suspicious Activity
indicators.statement: Accounts with privileged access are disabled or otherwise secured in response to suspicious activity.

indicators.name: Generating After Action Reports
indicators.statement: Incident after action reports are generated and lessons learned are persistently incorporated.

indicators.name: Reviewing Incident Response Procedures
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of documented incident response procedures is persistently reviewed.

controls.title: Automation Support for Availability of Information and Support

indicators.name: Reviewing Past Incidents
indicators.statement: Past incidents are persistently reviewed for patterns or vulnerabilities that were not previously apparent or identified.

indicators.name: Authorizing Log Access
indicators.statement: A least-privileged, role and attribute-based, and just-in-time access authorization model is used and persistently reviewed for access to log data based on organizationally defined data sensitivity.

indicators.name: Evaluating Configurations
indicators.statement: The configuration of machine-based information resources, especially infrastructure as code, is persistently evaluated and tested.

indicators.name: Logging Event Types
indicators.statement: A list of information resources and event types that will be logged, monitored, and audited is maintained and persistently reviewed to ensure these activities occur.

indicators.name: Operating SIEM Capability
indicators.statement: A Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) or similar system(s) is used and persistently reviewed for centralized, tamper-resistant logging of events, activities, and changes.

controls.title: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures
Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation
Automated Tools and Mechanisms for Real-time Analysis

indicators.name: Reviewing Logs
indicators.statement: Logs are persistently reviewed and audited.

controls.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting

indicators.name: Generating Inventories
indicators.statement: Authoritative sources are used to automatically generate real-time inventories of all information resources when needed.

controls.title: Automation Support for Accuracy and Currency
Automated Tools to Support Information Location

indicators.name: Reviewing Executive Support
indicators.statement: Executive support for achieving the provider's security goals is persistently reviewed and demonstrated.

indicators.name: Reviewing Investments in Security
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of the provider's investments in achieving security goals is persistently reviewed.

indicators.name: Reviewing Security in the SDLC
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of building security and privacy considerations into the Software Development Lifecycle and aligning with CISA Secure By Design principles is persistently reviewed.

indicators.name: Reviewing Vulnerability Disclosures
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of the provider's vulnerability disclosure program is persistently reviewed.

indicators.name: Aligning Backups with Objectives
indicators.statement: The alignment of machine-based information resource backups with defined recovery objectives is persistently reviewed.

indicators.name: Aligning Recovery Plan
indicators.statement: The alignment of recovery plans with defined recovery objectives is persistently reviewed.

indicators.name: Reviewing Recovery Objectives
indicators.statement: The desired Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO) are defined and persistently reviewed for alignment with the provider's business needs and capabilities.

indicators.name: Testing Recovery Capabilities
indicators.statement: The capability to recover from incidents and contingencies aligned with defined recovery objectives is persistently tested.

indicators.name: Mitigating Supply Chain Risk
indicators.statement: Persistently identify, review, and mitigate potential supply chain risks.

indicators.name: Monitoring Supply Chain Risk
indicators.statement: Third party software information resources are automatically monitored for upstream vulnerabilities using mechanisms that may include contractual notification requirements or active monitoring services.

indicators.name: Automating Configuration Management
indicators.statement: The configuration of machine-based information resources is managed using automation and persistently reviewed for drift.

controls.title: Automation Support for Accuracy and Currency

indicators.name: Automating Secret Management
indicators.statement: Management, protection, and regular rotation of digital keys, certificates, and other secrets is automated and persistently reviewed.

controls.title: Protection of Confidentiality and Integrity Using Encryption

indicators.name: Evaluating and Improving Security
indicators.statement: Information resources are persistently evaluated for opportunities to improve security and those improvements are persistently made.

controls.title: Automated Tools to Support Information Location

indicators.name: Preventing Residual Risk
indicators.statement: Plans, procedures, and the state of information resources are persistently reviewed after making changes to limit and remove unwanted residual elements that would likely negatively affect the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of federal customer data.

indicators.name: Removing Unwanted Data
indicators.statement: Unwanted federal customer data is removed promptly when requested by an agency in alignment with customer agreements, including from backups if appropriate; this typically applies when a customer spills information or when a customer seeks to remove information from a service due to a change in usage.

indicators.name: Securing Information
indicators.statement: Information is encrypted or otherwise secured from unwanted access or modification.

controls.title: Protection of Confidentiality and Integrity Using Encryption
Secure Name/Address Resolution Service (Recursive or Caching Resolver)
Architecture and Provisioning for Name/Address Resolution Service

indicators.name: Validating Communications
indicators.statement: The authenticity and integrity of communications between machine-based information resources is persistently validated using automation.

indicators.name: Validating Resource Integrity
indicators.statement: Use cryptographic methods to validate the integrity of machine-based information resources.

controls.title: Automation Support for Accuracy and Currency
